# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

# frozen_string_literal: true

require "base64"

module ActionDispatch
  module Session
    class MarshalCookieStore < CookieStore
      DEFAULT_OPTIONS = {
        key: "_session_id",
        path: "/",
        httponly: true,
        same_site: :lax
      }.freeze

      def initialize(app, options = {})
        super(app, DEFAULT_OPTIONS.merge(options))
      end

      private

      def unpacked_cookie_data(env)
        cookies = Rack::Request.new(env).cookies
        raw = cookies[options[:key].to_s] || cookies[options[:key].to_sym]
        return {} unless raw && !raw.empty?

        begin
          Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(raw)) || {}
        rescue StandardError
          {}
        end
      end

      def write_session(req, session_id, session_data, options)
        payload = Base64.strict_encode64(Marshal.dump(session_data))
        req.set_cookie(options[:key], cookie_options(options).merge(value: payload))
        session_id
      end

      def delete_session(req, session_id, options)
        req.delete_cookie(options[:key], cookie_options(options))
        generate_sid
      end
    end
  end
end

Rails.application.config.session_store :cookie_store, key: "_session_id"
Rails.application.config.middleware.swap ActionDispatch::Session::CookieStore, ActionDispatch::Session::MarshalCookieStore, key: Rails.application.config.session_options[:key] || "_session_id"
